title: The Bullet Holes That Aren't There
description: Push back when a colleague proposes copying every habit of successful startups. Tell the WWII airplane story.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: I read that all top startups did weekly retros. Should we?

templates.text: Maybe. But that argument has a survivorship bias problem.
templates.hint_zh: 也許吧。但這論點有倖存者偏差的問題。

templates.text: You're only looking at the survivors. Lots of failed startups also did weekly retros.
templates.hint_zh: 你只看到活下來的。很多倒掉的新創也做週會回顧。

templates.text: Sure. WWII engineers wanted to armor planes where the bullet holes were.
templates.hint_zh: 二戰工程師想加固飛機被子彈打中的地方。

templates.text: Backwards. Wald pointed out: those planes came back. Armor the spots without holes.
templates.hint_zh: 搞反了。Wald 指出那些飛機都飛回來了,該加固的是沒被打中的地方。

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Got it. So how do we apply this here?

templates.text: Find startups that died despite doing retros. If there are many, the practice doesn't explain success.
templates.hint_zh: 去找做了回顧但還是倒掉的新創。如果一堆,這做法就不是成功原因。

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Painful question. Let me dig and come back.

templates.text: Always. Look for the planes that didn't come back.
templates.hint_zh: 一直都是。找那些沒飛回來的飛機。
